programs.name: Medical Assistance (Medicaid) for Seniors/Disabled
programs.what_it_does: Provides health coverage including long-term medical and personal care for low-income seniors. Covers services to help seniors avoid institutional care through waivers.[4][9]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors and persons with disabilities who meet income eligibility.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; covers medical costs, long-term care.

programs.name: Home Services Program (HSP) Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Offers home and community-based services like personal care, home-delivered meals, and transportation as a Medicaid waiver. Enables seniors to receive care at home instead of nursing facilities.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Medicaid-eligible seniors aged 60+ needing nursing home level care but preferring home-based services.
programs.benefit_value: Personalized services based on assessed needs; prevents institutionalization.

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to eligible seniors in community settings. Integrates Medicare and Medicaid benefits for coordinated care.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 55+ certified for nursing home care but able to live safely in community.
programs.benefit_value: Full coverage of approved services including adult day health and transportation.

programs.name: Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), Qualifying Individual (QI)
programs.what_it_does: Pays Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments for eligible low-income seniors. Helps reduce out-of-pocket healthcare costs through state assistance.[1][9]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries meeting specific income and resource limits.
programs.benefit_value: Full/partial payment of Medicare Part A/B costs depending on program.

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides funds via Link card to purchase groceries for low-income households. Offers expanded eligibility and deductions for seniors including medical and housing expenses.[3][4]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ with income up to 200% federal poverty level; simplified recertification every 24 months.
programs.benefit_value: Monthly benefits based on income/household size; avg. $200+ per person.

programs.name: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers financial aid for energy bills especially during extreme weather. Helps low-income households manage heating/cooling costs.[4]
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants up to $1,500+ depending on fuel type and need.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Improves home energy efficiency through insulation, sealing, and efficiency upgrades. Reduces energy costs and improves comfort for low-income homes.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Free home upgrades; long-term energy savings.

programs.name: Senior Health Insurance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free health insurance counseling for Medicare beneficiaries and caregivers. Assists with Medicare options, appeals, and fraud prevention.[1][7][9]

programs.name: Community Care Program Home Delivered Meals
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to support independence. Part of broader in-home and community-based care services.[1][4]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals.
programs.benefit_value: 5-14 meals/week based on eligibility; promotes nutrition and reduces isolation.

programs.name: Community Care Program Caregiver Support/Respite
programs.what_it_does: Offers respite services and support for family caregivers of seniors. Provides temporary relief and training to prevent caregiver burnout.[1][4]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of older adults receiving community care services.
programs.benefit_value: Respite hours, counseling, and financial aid as needed.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time community service jobs with training for low-income seniors. Aims to increase employability and income.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage pay for 20 hours/week training positions.

programs.name: Illinois Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program (LTCOP)
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities to protect rights and improve quality of life. Investigates complaints and provides support.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, assisted living, and other long-term care settings.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy and complaint resolution services.

programs.name: Legal Assistance for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides legal aid to low-income seniors for issues like benefits, housing, and abuse. Delivered through Area Agencies on Aging and partners.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Free or low-cost legal consultations and representation.
